What is retrieval-augmented generation?: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is a framework that combines a retrieval step with a generative language model to produce grounded, factual answers from a document corpus.
How does ChromaDB store embeddings?: ChromaDB stores embeddings as dense vectors in an HNSW index on disk, persisting data to a local directory for efficient approximate nearest-neighbour search.
What is the role of a reranker in a RAG: A reranker is a cross-encoder model that rescores the initial retrieval candidates by jointly encoding the query and each passage, yielding a more precise relevance ranking.
What embedding model is used by default?: The default embedding model is OpenAI text-embedding-3-small, producing 1536-dimensional vectors.
Explain the chunking strategy used in th: Documents are split using a recursive character text splitter that respects a configurable token budget per chunk and maintains overlap to preserve context across boundaries.
What LLM providers are supported?: The system supports OpenAI (gpt-4.1-mini by default) and Ollama for fully local inference.
How are citations generated?: Citations are built from the metadata attached to each retrieved chunk, including the source file name, page number, and an excerpt of the passage used in the answer.
What file formats can be uploaded?: The system supports PDF, DOCX, TXT, and Markdown (MD) files.
How does the system prevent hallucinatio: The system prompt explicitly instructs the LLM to answer only from the provided context passages and to state when it lacks sufficient information instead of fabricating facts.
What is the Ragas evaluation framework?: Ragas is an open-source framework for evaluating RAG pipelines on metrics such as faithfulness, answer relevancy, context recall, and context precision.
